# MyGrind

> MyGrind is a training journal app for youth baseball and softball players (ages 13-18). Players log practices, games, stats, arm care, and academics (NCAA core-course GPA + eligibility), so they develop on purpose and have a record when it is time to get recruited. Built by Coach Mike Young, who has spent 35 years in the game as a coach, trainer, and mentor. Baseball and softball, players and parents.

Brand: MyGrind (My Grind Sports LLC). Voice: a real coach talking straight to families. The athlete is always "your player." MyGrind covers both baseball and softball, boys and girls.

## App
- [MyGrind](https://www.mygrindapp.com/): The training journal app for baseball and softball players. Tracks practice, games, stats, arm care, and academics/NCAA eligibility.
- [MyGrind Weekly (free newsletter)](https://www.mygrindapp.com/newsletter): A free weekly email for players and parents on development, recruiting, and the realities of youth ball.

## Free guides (blog)
- [Youth Bat Sizing Guide: What Size Bat Your Player Actually Needs](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/youth-bat-sizing-guide.html): Fit beats age charts; two tests decide it. Hip test for length (knob between hip bone and mid thigh) and the one-arm hold for weight (bat held out one-handed 15-20 seconds without the barrel dipping); when two sizes pass, take the smaller because bat speed beats bat size. Typical sizes: ages 5-7 24-26in drop -11 to -13, 8-9 26-28in -10 to -12, 10-11 28-30in -10/-11, 12-13 30-32in -8 to -10, high school baseball 31-33in BBCOR -3. Drop = length minus weight. Certification stamps must match the league: USA Baseball for Little League and rec, USSSA for travel, BBCOR for high school and college; fastpitch softball has its own USA Softball/USSSA/NSA marks and most players stay at drop -10/-11 into high school. Under about age 12 fit matters more than premium bat technology. The too-big buy-it-to-grow-into-it bat is the most common mistake and hurts the swing. Applies to baseball and fastpitch softball.
- [Youth Pitcher Arm Care: A Coach's Guide to Protecting Growing Arms](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/youth-pitcher-arm-care.html): A young arm is not a small adult arm; until growth plates close, the plate is weaker than the ligament, which is why kids get Little League elbow (a growth plate overuse injury: inner-elbow pain, lost accuracy or velocity, always stop throwing and see a doctor) where adults tear the UCL. Pitch Smart daily limits roughly 50 pitches at ages 7-8 up to 105 at 17-18, counted across ALL teams combined; 2-3 months per year with no overhead throwing; never pitch fatigued; no pitching plus catching the same day. Tommy John surgery does not add velocity and teens are its fastest growing patient group. Softball windmill is easier on the elbow but not injury-free and softball has no universal pitch counts, so families must count. Applies to baseball and softball, boys and girls.
- [Creatine for Teenage Athletes: A Coach's Honest Answer](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/creatine-for-teenage-athletes.html): Most teenage athletes do not need creatine; sleep, meals, and hydration come first. Creatine is not a steroid; adult research is strong but teen research is thin, so the decision belongs with the family pediatrician. If a family chooses it: plain creatine monohydrate, NSF Certified for Sport, no loading, around age 16+ with basics locked in. Applies equally to softball and baseball, girls and boys.
- [New NCAA Eligibility Rules 2026: The Age-Based Clock](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/ncaa-eligibility-rule-change-2026.html): The NCAA replaced five-years-to-play-four with an age-based eligibility clock on June 23, 2026. Division I only; clock starts at the earlier of full-time enrollment or the academic year after the athlete turns 19; medical redshirt removed; transition period through fall 2027 enrollees; core-course GPA and academic eligibility unchanged.
- [Private vs Public School: How to Choose a High School](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/choosing-high-school-baseball-softball.html): Private vs public, transfers, sections and divisions, and why playing time matters more than the name on the jersey.
- [When Should You Switch Travel Teams?](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/when-to-switch-travel-teams.html): Good reasons vs bad reasons to switch teams, and how to tell a player problem from a parent problem.
- [NCAA Eligibility and GPA](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/ncaa-eligibility-gpa-guide.html): How NCAA core-course GPA works, the sliding scale, and what actually counts toward eligibility.
- [Youth Baseball & Softball Training Calendar](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/youth-baseball-softball-training-calendar.html): A month-by-month training calendar with rest windows, the strength-building months, and tryout season.
- [Baseball & Softball Stats Worth Tracking](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/baseball-softball-stats-to-track.html): Which stats actually matter at each age, and which ones create false anxiety.
- [After 12U: Travel Ball or Patience](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/after-12u-travel-ball-or-patience.html): The jump to the bigger field and the decisions families face after 12U.
- [Travel Ball Then and Now](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/travel-ball-then-and-now.html): How travel baseball and softball became a $28B business in 25 years, the cost to young arms, and the recruiting truth.
- [What Is a Training Journal](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/what-is-a-baseball-training-journal.html): What a baseball/softball training journal is and why it speeds development.
- [Softball Training Journal](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/softball-training-journal.html): Keeping a training journal for fastpitch softball.
- [How to Keep a Training Journal](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/how-to-keep-a-training-journal.html): A simple habit for logging practices, games, and reps.

## Resources
- [Coach's Picks: Books & Gear](https://www.mygrindapp.com/picks): The baseball and softball books and training gear Coach actually recommends for players and parents.
- [Blog index](https://www.mygrindapp.com/blog/): All free guides in one place.

## About
- Built by Coach Mike Young, 35 years in baseball as a coach, trainer, and mentor. MyGrind serves youth baseball and softball families nationally, including girls in baseball.
- Contact: coach@mygrindapp.com
